english: there is no room for it; bre lṅa soṅ-bai bum-pa (bre lnga song ba'i bum pa) a can holding five quarts Dzl. II. pf. bsaṅs (bsangs), fut. bsaṅ (bsang), imp. soṅ(s) (song(s)), to empty, remove, carry or take away, W. stones, earth etc., but gen. (with or without rkyag-pa (rkyag pa)) to go to stool, to ease nature, B. and col.

1. the lower, the inferior part of a thing, rtse-śod (rtse shod) upper and lower part, top and bottom C.; rgya-śod (rgya shod) Wdn. a low tract of land, with a milder climate, where e.g. apricots are thriving, opp. to pʻu-lhágs (phu lhags) elevated cold region, scarcely fit for the cultivation of corn and barley; śod-du (shod du) to or towards the bottom, down, downwards, C., *śọ̈-du báb-pa* (*shod du bab pa*) to descend, come down, *śọ̈-nẹ dzég-pa* (*shod nas 'dzeg pa*) to ascend C. — 2. imp. of ˚čád-pa ('chad pa), bśád-pa (bshad pa); yet cf. also:
pf. bśád-pa (bshad pa), 1. to say, to declare C. — 2. to comb Cs.
a fib, falsehood, lie, smrá-ba (smra ba), W. *gyáb-če* (*rgyab ce*), to tell a lie.
loose, soft, as leaves etc. W.

pf. (b)śoms ((b)shoms), bśams (bshams), fut. bśam (bsham), imp. (b)śom(s) ((b)shom(s)), to prepare, make ready, arrange, put in order, fit out, gnas (gnas) lodgings, gdan (gdan) a seat, stón-mo (ston mo) a festive entertainment, bzö́n-pa (bzhon pa) a carriage, i.e. to have the horses put to Dzl.; dpúṅ-gi tsogs (dpung gi tshogs) an army Dzl. — śóm-ra (shom ra) 1. preparation, arrangement, fitting out, śóm-ra byéd-pa (shom ra byed pa) = śóm-pa (shom pa) C., so also Cs.; but Sch.: 2. śóm-ra (shom ra) state, pomp, splendour, with byéd-pa (byed pa) to show off, to dress smartly, śóm-čan (shom can) stately, grand (?).
1. v. ˚čór-ba ('chor ba). — 2. to measure Mil., v. gśór-ba (gshor ba).
1. intercalation, insertion Cs., zla-śól (zla shol) intercalary month. — 2. Cs: pres. tense of bśól-ba (bshol ba) q.v.
Sch.: 'a species of willow', v. gśól-po (gshol po).

1. almost always in conjunction with gčig (gcig), the other, of two, e.g. bud-méd gčig-śós (bud med gcig shos) the other woman Dzl. — 2. Zam. तर, col. śog (shog), a termination indicating the comparative or superlative degree: čuṅ-śós (chung shos), gžon-śós (gzhon shos) the younger, the youngest, of two or of several, btsún-mo lṅa-brgyái čuṅ-śós (btsun mo lnga brgya'i chung shos) Pth.; *riṅ-śóg* (*ring shog*) W. the tallest; yun-riṅ-śós (yun ring shos) Thgy. the most long-lived; drin-čé-śós (drin che shos) the principal benefactress, cf. drin (drin); nad čé-śós rgyás-nad ˚tẹ́bs-pas (nad che shos rgyas nad 'thebs pas) because one is suffering under the chief disease, viz. old age, Thgy.

Ssk., = dpal (dpal) glory, magnificence; magnificent, splendid, grand; śri-ri (shri ri) Mil., pr. n., a naked mountain in a sandy plain, about a ten day's journey to the west of Tashi-lhunpo, covered with monasteries, and perh. on that account considered as nyams-mtsár-ba (nyams mtshar ba). śri-kánḍa (shri kan da) v. dpál-gyi dum-bu (dpal gyi dum bu).
Ssk., also śo-lo-ka (sho lo ka), strophe, stanza, esp. one consisting of four catalectic trochaical dimeters.

I. also gśạ-ma (gsha' ma), bśạ-ma (bsha' ma), worthy, becoming, fitting, suitable, ˚tsé-ba mi gśại ('tshe ba mi gsha'i) as it is unworthy, unbecoming, improper, to persecute (others) Dzl.; *de kyọ́d-la śa yod* (*de khyod la sha yod*) or *śạ-če yod* (*gsha' ce yod*) Ld. that serves you right. — 2. righteous, upright, honest, good, = skyón-med-pa (skyon med pa), C.; blo gśạ-ma (blo gsha' ma) an upright, true heart, Thgy.; *lẹ śạ-ma jhé-pa* (*las gsha' ma byed pa*) C. to perform a work faithfully, in good earnest. II. only, merely; mere, nothing but, (= śá-stag (sha stag)) C.
S.g., Wdn., C. tin, W. kar-ya (dkar ya); yet cf. ža-nye (zha nye).
a long skirt or coat-tail Sch.

right, justice, Lex. and esp. W.; gśags byed-pa (gshags byed pa) Lex. to investigate the rightfulness (of an action), = *ge dig pé-če* (*dge sdig phye ce*) W.; *ṭim-śág táṅ-če* (*khrims shags btang ce*) to administer justice, to sit in judgment *ṭim-śág taṅ-kán* (*khrims shags btang mkhan*) judge, *śag (go-) lóg táṅ-če* (*shags (go) log btang ce*) to warp justice, to judge contrary to justice and right; *ṭim-śág žú-če* (*khrims shags zhu ce*) to go to law, to bring an action, *śág-pon* (*shags dpon*) superior judge, chief-justice, W. — bka-gśágs (bka' gshags) v. bka (bka') extr.
